title: Rhaphiolepis rubra
taxonomy: Rhaphiolepis rubra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, which includes other rose-like plants. This plant is native to China and is known for its red flowers and glossy leaves. Rhaphiolepis rubra is further classified as a member of the genus Rhaphiolepis, which contains around 15 species of flowering plants. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens due to its attractive flowers and foliage. In terms of its broader taxonomic classification, Rhaphiolepis rubra is a member of the order Rosales, which includes many other rose and rose-like plants.
uses: Rhaphiolepis rubra has been traditionally used in various folk remedies, although its effectiveness is largely anecdotal. Its roots, leaves, and flowers are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and fever. The plant's bark is also used to treat gum diseases and skin conditions. More research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

side_effects: Rhaphiolepis rubra, also known as the hollywood hedging plant, is generally considered safe for use in landscaping and horticulture. However, as with any plant, there may be some potential side effects to consider. If ingested, Rhaphiolepis rubra's leaves or flowers may cause gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and vomiting.
